Name: adaptive_precision_scaling
Title: Adaptive Precision Scaling in Kernel Execution for Real-time Inference
Short Hypothesis: Dynamically adjusting numerical precision during kernel execution can enhance throughput and reduce latency without significant accuracy loss in inference tasks.
Related Work: While many studies focus on static optimizations of GPU kernels or performance forecasting (e.g., NeuSight), none investigate the runtime adaptability of precision in a kernel execution context. This approach distinguishes itself by directly affecting real-time performance metrics on hardware rather than making theoretical predictions.
Abstract: In this research proposal, we explore the potential of Adaptive Precision Scaling (APS) in kernel execution to enhance the efficiency of deep learning inference tasks on GPUs. Current methods often rely on static precision settings, which can lead to suboptimal trade-offs between accuracy and performance. Our hypothesis is that dynamically adjusting the numerical precision based on real-time profiling can significantly improve throughput and reduce latency. By implementing a system that monitors kernel performance and adjusts precision levels on-the-fly, we aim to optimize resource usage while maintaining accuracy. This approach will be evaluated through a series of experiments measuring wall-clock latency, throughput, and energy consumption across various models and tasks. We expect to show that APS can lead to a notable improvement in performance metrics, providing a practical solution for deploying deep learning models efficiently on commodity hardware.
Experiments: Implement a baseline kernel execution with fixed precision and measure its performance metrics (latency, throughput).
Develop an APS system that monitors kernel performance and dynamically adjusts precision during execution.
Run comparative experiments between the baseline and APS-enabled kernels across multiple inference tasks (e.g., CNNs, transformers).
Collect detailed profiling data, including occupancy, achieved bandwidth, and arithmetic intensity.
Evaluate metrics such as p50/p99 latency, throughput under batching, and energy per token.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of implementing real-time monitoring and adjustment may introduce overhead.
Potential accuracy degradation if precision adjustments are not managed correctly.
Limited generalizability to all types of deep learning models or tasks.
